data.title: Tether considering gold supply chain investment, holding $8.7B in physical gold and a $1.4B market cap gold-backed crypto.
data.description: Tether currently holds physical gold worth $8.7 billion and has developed a cryptocurrency backed by gold with a market capitalization of $1.4 billion.

data.title: Samsung reveals vision for "AI Home: Future Living, Now" at IFA 2025, aiming for homes to adapt to needs and care for what matters.
data.description: In Berlin, Samsung Electronics unveiled its vision for "AI Home: Future Living, Now" at Innovation For All (IFA) 2025. The concept aims to bring AI experiences to life currently, catering to all individuals, rather than a limited group. Samsung emphasizes that they are not just conceptualizing AI's future but actively integrating it into daily routines. The goal is to have homes equipped to comprehend users, adjust to their requirements, and prioritize what matters most to them. This move showcases Samsung's commitment to making AI accessible and practical.

data.title: HBAR price drops to $0.22 due to technical breakdown, down by 4%. Sibos 2025 participation may attract institutional investors.
data.description: The HBAR token of Hedera is currently trading at $0.22, experiencing a 4% decrease due to technical issues. However, there is anticipation that the participation in Sibos 2025 may attract institutional investors, potentially boosting interest in the cryptocurrency.

data.title: Designer Brands likely to report lower Q2 earnings as analysts forecast 22 cents per share, down from 29 cents last year. Shares closed at $4.41, gaining 11.1%.
data.description: Designer Brands Inc. (NYSE: DBI) is scheduled to announce its second-quarter earnings before the market opens on Tuesday, September 9. Analysts predict earnings of 22 cents per share for the Columbus, Ohio-headquartered company, a decrease from 29 cents per share reported in the same period last year. The company is expected to reveal quarterly revenue of $737.85 million, down from $771.9 million a year ago. In a previous update on June 10, Designer Brands reported disappointing Q1 results below expectations and retracted its FY25 guidance due to uncertainties in the macroeconomic environment. Following this news, Designer Brands shares surged by 11.1% to close at $4.41. For more details, the full article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Inlyte Energy appoints three executives including former CTO of GE Energy Storage to leadership team for commercialization.
data.description: On September 5, 2025, Inlyte Energy, based in San Leandro, California, revealed the addition of three new executives to its leadership team. One of the appointments includes Sandor Hollo, the previous Chief Technology Officer at GE Energy Storage. Inlyte Energy specializes in producing iron-sodium battery energy storage systems.

data.title: Slovakia's construction industry to grow by 1.6% in 2025, with a projected AAGR of 4.2% during 2026-2029 driven by infrastructure, industrial, and energy investments. Government focus on renewable energy.
data.description: Dublin - The Slovakia Construction Market is projected to experience growth of 1.6% in real terms in the year 2025. This growth is attributed to increased manufacturing activities and investments in energy and infrastructure projects. The market analysis report, titled "Slovakia Construction Market Size, Trends, and Forecasts by Sector - Commercial, Industrial, Infrastructure, Energy and Utilities, Institutional and Residential Market Analysis to 2029 (H2 2025)," has been recently provided by ResearchAndMarkets.com. The report offers insights into various sectors including commercial, industrial, infrastructure, energy and utilities, institutional, and residential construction markets in Slovakia. This positive outlook reflects the ongoing developments and investments in the country's construction industry.

data.title: New Fortress Energy Inc. reported Q2 2025 results: EBITDA $(4M), net loss $557M, asset impairments $699M, gain from sale $473M.
data.description: New York-based New Fortress Energy Inc. (Nasdaq: NFE) released its financial figures for the second quarter of 2025. The company reported an Adjusted EBITDA of $(4) million, with a net loss of $557 million during the same period. They also noted substantial non-cash impairments amounting to $699 million, as well as a gain of $473 million from the sale of their Jamaican operations. The Earnings Per Share (EPS) stood at $(2.02) on a fully diluted basis for the second quarter of 2025. Additionally, the company disclosed a total cash balance.

data.title: Peter Schiff celebrates as gold hits new all-time high at $3,600, criticizes Bitcoin for lagging behind.
data.description: Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) is currently in a consolidation phase, leading to a debate regarding its performance compared to gold. Peter Schiff, a critic of Bitcoin, observed that Gold (NYSE: GLD) reached new record highs at approximately $3,600, while Bitcoin has not shown a similar response. Schiff highlighted that Bitcoin is trailing behind gold by about 15% from its peak in 2021. This disparity has raised concerns about Bitcoin losing momentum, especially as the Federal Reserve is considering rate cuts amidst persistent inflation. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: StablecoinX boosts funding to $890M for Ethena's ENA treasury.
data.description: In recent findings, it has been revealed that approximately 8 million children under the age of 5 died in the past year due to preventable causes. As reported, this is a slight decrease from previous years but still highlights the urgent need for improved healthcare and nutrition for young children around the world. The data also shows that around 45% of these deaths occurred within the first 28 days of life, indicating a crucial need for better maternal and newborn care. Furthermore, it was noted that sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia were the regions with the highest child mortality rates, emphasizing the ongoing disparities in healthcare access and quality. These statistics underscore the importance of continued efforts to address the root causes of child mortality and work towards ensuring a healthier future for all children globally.

data.title: Dow falls on weak jobs report; Nvidia dips, Tesla rises. Nasdaq gains. Live news coverage details movements.
data.description: On Friday, the Dow Jones index experienced a decline following a disappointing August jobs report. Nvidia shares saw a decrease in value, whereas Tesla stocks showed an increase. The stock market today witnessed the Dow sliding as the Nasdaq achieved weekly gains. Nvidia broke a significant level, while Tesla observed a rise.

data.title: Microsoft 365 is weakest link in healthcare email breaches, accounting for 52% of all breaches, a significant increase from 43% last year. Paubox report details trend in 107 breaches in first half of 2025.
data.description: In San Francisco, cybercriminals are on the rise, especially targeting Microsoft 365 environments. The globally popular business email platform now makes up 52% of healthcare email breaches, a significant increase from 43% in the previous year. This concerning trend is highlighted in Paubox's latest report, "2025 mid-year email breach data reveals no signs of slowing down," which examined 107 healthcare data breaches related to emails in the first half of 2025. This data points to a growing threat to email security in the healthcare sector.

data.title: Lido DAO token (LDO) hovers at $1.21 (-0.21%), as governance vote impacts market sentiment, mixed signals observed.
data.description: The current trading price of LDO stands at $1.21, reflecting a decrease of 0.21% for the day. Investors are facing mixed signals due to the ongoing governance upgrade vote. This situation is compounded by bearish technical indicators and a neutral position of the Relative Strength Index (RSI).

data.title: Guidewire Software beats Q4 estimates, reports $84 cents per share earnings, $356.57M sales. Forecasts FY2026 sales of $1.385B-$1.405B. CEO pleased with fourth-quarter results.
data.description: Guidewire Software, Inc. announced its fourth-quarter results, surpassing analyst expectations. The company's quarterly earnings stood at $0.84 per share, exceeding the consensus estimate of $0.64 per share. Additionally, quarterly sales reached $356.57 million, beating the analyst estimate of $337.858 million. Guidewire Software forecasts sales for fiscal year 2026 to be in the range of $1.385 billion to $1.405 billion, as opposed to the estimated $1.184 billion. CEO Mike Rosenbaum expressed satisfaction with the quarter, citing the completion of 19 cloud deals and exceeding $1 billion in ARR. A notable achievement was securing a significant 10-year agreement with a leading Tier-1 insurer, demonstrating platform maturity.

data.title: China celebrates V-Day with grand military parade in Beijing marking 80th anniversary of victory against Japanese Aggression.
data.description: In Beijing on September 3, a significant Victory Day military parade took place to honor the 80th anniversary of the triumph in China's People's War of Resistance Against Japanese Aggression and World War II. The event marked a crucial milestone in history, emphasizing China's resilience and victory. The parade witnessed a grand display of military strength and precision, showcasing advanced weaponry and equipment. Spectators and viewers from across the globe were able to witness the grandeur of the event, commemorating the sacrifices made during the conflict. The parade highlighted China's commitment to honoring its history and ensuring peace and stability in the region. This celebration attracted a massive audience, with an estimated attendance of over 100,000 people, both in person and through various media platforms. The event received widespread coverage, drawing attention to China's pivotal role in the victory over aggression and its enduring stance for peace.

data.title: "CBS revamps editing policy for 'Face the Nation' after admin backlash. No more edits on recorded interviews."
data.description: Starting this Sunday, the show "Face the Nation" announced a significant change in their policy regarding recorded interviews with newsmakers. The network will no longer edit these interviews following accusations from the administration of deceptively editing an appearance by the homeland security secretary. This decision comes after the administration raised concerns about the editing practices of the show and marks a shift towards greater transparency in their reporting. The move aims to ensure that the conversations are presented in their original context without any alterations. This change will apply to all future interviews aired on the show, emphasizing the importance of maintaining the integrity of the discussions.

data.title: Estonia construction industry to grow by 3.5% in 2025. AAGR of 4.7% till 2028. Focus on renewable energy, green hydrogen, transport infrastructure projects.
data.description: A recently released report on the Estonia construction market indicates a projected growth of 3.5% in 2025. This growth is expected to be driven by increasing building construction activities and investments in commercial, industrial, infrastructure, energy and utilities, institutional, and residential sectors. The market analysis forecasts trends and developments in various sectors including commercial, industrial, infrastructure, energy and utilities, institutional, and residential markets until 2029. The report source, ResearchAndMarkets.com, suggests that these factors will contribute to steady growth in the Estonian construction industry.

data.title: NexGold modifies Equity Incentive Plan. Company details undisclosed.
data.description: In Toronto on September 5, 2025, NexGold Mining Corp. (TSXV: NEXG; OTCQX: NXGCF) disclosed changes to its Omnibus Equity Incentive Plan. The company, also known as NexGold, altered specific provisions within the Plan.
